Let's get on our feet, get on our feet, get on our feet. Come on now, we gotta work off some dinner. And let's hear it for Dan Kennedy. Really loud, come on now. She's looking a lot of the night. She's gonna keep this thing going. Really loud. There you go. Thank you, sir. All right, we have much work to do. And after a few little remarks, we will be moving very quickly. You should have a cheat sheet that was handed out to you that will help you. Good. Very, very good. Okay. So here's the preliminary remarks. We're gonna deal with the first two. And then we're gonna do the second one. And then we're gonna do the third one. Here's the preliminary remarks. We're gonna deal with seven secrets of mind control copy for the selling of info products and services. And the preface remark is this. It is possible that some of you will be made uncomfortable. More so those of you who are like the virgins to this than the ones who've been hanging around for a while, but it is possible that some of you will be made uncomfortable by some of the techniques within these seven secrets because the underlying premise is one of the most dangerous premises ever invented by man. It is that the end justifies the means. And in many cases, that has not worked out very well. But it is necessary to sell to the maximum and to use the most powerful techniques there are in copy to sell info products for you to feel okay in your situation with the end justifying your means. I, it is beyond me to have a big ethics class with you. But I will just forewarn you that I am acting under the assumption that everybody here is an honorable ethical person who is selling good stuff that will be so beneficial to people that every extreme mechanism used to sell it is a good thing. If that's not true, it's up to you to figure out how far to back off from the maximum effectiveness. The reason extreme techniques are more necessary in this business than in many is that we actually are to a great extent selling nothing. We are essentially selling people on their own assets, skills, talents, capabilities, and opportunities that through whatever it is we do, we improve their health, we improve their wealth, we improve whatever, that they are going to be better at that. They are actually, when they invest with you, they are really investing in themselves. It is not like selling a magic slicer-dicer machine where there's a machine. And for your money, you actually walk out of the room with a machine. In our case, if you walk out with anything, you walk out with stuff that has to be translated into action in order for it to have any value. So our job is much tougher. And that requires us to use tougher techniques. So to start with, there are four vital principles. Number one is, in selling and copy that sells information, we, ironically, do not empower the prospect to think. In the selling phase of what we do, we are not in the business of getting anybody to think at all. In fact, better that they don't. We are not empowering them. We are actually making them powerless for a brief period of time to engage in any thinking, except the thinking that we direct them to do that serves our purpose, which is selling them what it is that we are there by media, in person, however it is that we are there to sell them. Linked premise, we do not seek rational thought. We seek emotional response at the exclusion of rational thought. Our buyers, for the most part, have to suspend logic. They have to suspend skepticism. They have to suspend their past experience, often in order to make a purchase from us. And to do that, we are all about emotional response, not at all about logic. And everything that even appears to be logical, and I'll point it out to you in all the copy examples as we go along tonight, is really all about an emotional response. Very important. Three, ironically, we do not educate any more than is absolutely necessary to make the sale. We manipulate, we motivate, we do not need an informed consumer. We need an excited consumer. One of the biggest copy mistakes in this business is dispensing information and education and being a teacher, instead of being a salesperson, up until the time that we have their money and now we should begin to teach. So there is a tendency, speakers make the mistake a lot, but it transfers into media and it transfers into copy. There is a tendency, for example, if you have a marketing document that you call a special report, and you offer it and deliver a report to somebody, there is a tendency for people to think that because they called it a report, it's supposed to have information in it. No. It is supposed to appear to have information in it. It is supposed to be perceived as having information in it. It is supposed to be perceived as being educational. If it is actually educational and informative, all that does is get in the way of the sale. And we are about selling stuff first, educating after the sale, not as part of the sale. Fourth, there is a sales purpose behind every single piece of copy. If it does not further the sale, it does not earn its space on the page, it does not earn its minutes in the video, it does not earn its minutes on stage. Speakers, for example, will get bored with what they do. I talked about this at lunch with people. And the temptation is to put in new stuff, to talk about something topical that interests them, to do like I did at the beginning this morning and reference the CNN interview with Zuckerberg's sister. Well, that is only permissible because I had no purpose this morning other than to fill time, be perhaps informative, entertain, have everybody be happy. If I had been trying to sell something, that would be an unforgivable sin. Because there was nothing about it that would have advanced the sale and in order to do it today, I would have had to take something out of that script that was there already that advanced the sale to make room for it. So in indulging myself, I would be jeopardizing the sale. People do the same thing in media. They tend to put in a story that they like to tell, but it really doesn't move the sale forward. We don't have that luxury. So everything is about moving forward the sale. And it is almost always tied to an emotional factor. Now there are some lists that exist for you of the basic E factors. You don't have to jot these down. You probably have the book therein. So one place to find them is in the marketing to the affluent book. The most basic E factors are fear. We're going to spend a lot of time tonight on fear. Guilt, greed, pride, and love, or if you prefer, lust. It sort of depends on whether you're on your first marriage, second marriage, fifth marriage, where you are in that whole continuum. And then also in that book you'll find some E factors that happen to be specific to affluent individuals. Whatever your target market, there will be E factors specific to that target market, but the big general ones apply. Importantly, we do better with the dark than with the light. And most of what you will see tonight is in the dark category, not in the light category. And I will convincingly demonstrate to you that if you have a choice and you are not able to split test, you are always better off dragging them into the darkness than you are showing them happy things and light. And I will demonstrate to you. So we begin with the first of the seven factors. It is shame. And so no one wants to live in loserville. There are a whole lot of people who don't want to do enough about it to get out. We tend not to get money from them. But no one wants to be there at all. Our buyer has a stronger reaction to it than most. He fears and loathes it and he can be motivated to do something about it. This is called negative motivation. And of all of the options of how to sell people on improvement, opportunity, education, information, change, negative motivation works so much better than positive motivation. Here is my favorite shame ad. This is from 1923. It is the only one I think I will show you tonight that is not actually an information marketing industry example, but it is so good. I had to show it to you. This actually is for a paint and varnish products company that sells paint to paint your house with. Here is the ad. So two people stand outside a house and the headline is, Jones must be broke. And the copy is all about his house looks so bad. Clearly the poor sucker must be broke. That is the only reason he has let his house have chip paint and so forth. It is an ad to send in a coupon to get coupons to buy paint with. You can't have a stronger shame headline than Jones must be broke. 1923, great copywriter. Confidence, consideration and respect surrounds the family whose home inside and out bears the look of prosperity that only the proper use of paint and varnish can impart. Isn't that good? So there is three chief problems with our business. So one is, we are only going to deal with one tonight, but just so you know what all three of them are. I have just had four conversations about it before I came up here. Everybody understands the first one. So the first chief evil of our business is the cost of new customer acquisition. That is the first chief problem in our business that everybody is bedeviled by. If you are not yet bedeviled by it, you will be as soon as you try and get enough growth. And it manifests itself all different ways. The cost of filling a seat in a preview seminar, which is the conversation I had in the back of the room before we came up here with several people. The cost of getting traffic to a site in sufficient quantity that you can build a business from it, etc. etc. That is a subject not for tonight. The second evil is the ever rising clutter and competition of free content. That is a topic not for tonight, but just so you know it is the second evil. The third is one a lot of people don't understand. And it's very hard for anybody in this room to get it. And that is the absence of legitimate ambition in the overwhelming majority of every population. So if you sell to business owners, for example, and you are a business owner, you are an ambitious business owner. You're here. You spent money. You got on an airplane. You dealt with disappointed children at whose game you will not be at. You made sacrifices because you are ambitious. You get up pretty much every day being ambitious. It's very hard for you to think that there's a bunch of people in business or a bunch of people in your business who in fact are not ambitious at all. They may speak ambition, but they don't do ambition. And we mostly can only make money on people who do ambition. In business owners, there's like three categories. There's the be your own boss crowd. That's the people who really just want not to have a boss. So that's often say the auto mechanic who has become the owner of an auto repair shop. He did not do that because he had ambition. His ambition was limited to not having a boss. And he has the delusion that if he just owned the thing, instead of working for the guy that owned the thing, he would be free of a boss. He discovers, of course, to his dire disappointment that he now has a thousand bosses instead of one because there's customers. And he has one primary boss who's completely dysfunctional and incompetent. And he also has a really crappy employee. They are one and the same. They are him. He can't quit and he can't fire him. So he's in a world of hurt. But that does not translate into the kind of ambition that we get to leverage. The overwhelming majority can be more motivated by ambition to escape the unwanted. Then ambition to grow, improve, excel, build an empire, be an entrepreneur, be healthy, be fit, etc. So it's important you build into your messaging things that are related to the ambition to escape the unwanted. It exists waiting to be poked in a greater number of people and it is easier to stimulate in a greater number of people. So we have to use it. If you only sell to one percenters, it's very hard to make that business work. In fact, the difference between even high-income individuals and wealthy individuals is high-income individuals only monetize their customers. Wealthy people monetize all their unconverted leads. They monetize everybody who they touch, everybody who touches them one way or another. In order for us to do that, we have to appeal to the actual ambition that a large percentage of the people we talk to, write to, communicate with, start out with, which is the ambition to escape the unwanted, not the ambition to be better, to be bigger, to be richer, to be healthier, to be thinner. It is the ambition to escape the unwanted. If you want a the czarist for it, here it is. People don't want to be left out. They don't want to be left behind. They don't want to be uncool. They don't want to be stupid. They don't want to be attached to a loser or a losing argument. This is why in politics, by the way, the perception of who's going to win begins to affect how people vote dramatically. So all the late, the so-called undecided voters, that's why they're undecided. They're not undecided because they haven't yet got enough information in order to make a decision. It's two weeks from the election. Our election cycles are now permanent. We're in the 2016 presidential election cycle. Now, by the time we get to two weeks before the election, how could any person possibly be? But you hear about all these undecided voters. Well, this is what they're undecided about. They're undecided about who's going to win. Because they want to vote for the winner. So they could say, they picked a winner. They don't want to be associated with the loser. There are exceptions. There's a guy by neighborhood who's got a Jimmy Carter bumper sticker on his car. But he's an exception. You've got to give him credit for being stubborn. Embarrassed, people don't want to be embarrassed. They don't want to feel foolish. They don't want to feel incompetent. They don't want to feel weak and impotent. They don't want to be looked down upon. They don't want to be unwanted, pitied, gossiped about, unattractive, or old. And they have ambition to escape these conditions. And they are far easier to motivate about that escape than they are about the positive alternatives to all of this. It's also important to understand that even successful people, even if you sell to successful people, they are hypersensitive to criticism and imagine critical thoughts of others. Almost everybody's walking around wondering what somebody else and somebody's else is thinking about them. For the most part, nobody's thinking about them at all. So most of this is in their imagination. It's not in reality, but it is there. They are wondering about that. And so you can use it if you wish. I'll show you a couple of classics. This is from 1922. You could tweak it and run it today. How little social errors ruined their biggest chance. This is a full-page ad, ran in newspapers in the 1920s. It's got a coupon in it. It's a lead generation ad for a home study course. I'm just going to read you a few of the subheads and the captions. Bad mistakes are made. The Kratons suffer keen humiliation. Excuse me. He knew that others were watching them, reading in their embarrassment their lack of social knowledge. I'll read your paragraph. We're doing copy. I am going to have to read you a few things as we go along tonight. But then something happened. Violet noticed that Mrs. Roberts had glanced at her husband and frowned ever so slightly. She wondered what was wrong. Perhaps it was incorrect to cut a lettuce with a knife. Perhaps Ted should not have used his fork that way. In her embarrassment, she dropped her knife and bent down to pick it up at the same time that the butler did. Oh, it was humiliating. Unbearable. They should never have come. They didn't know what to do. They did not know how to act. You could tweak it. You could run it today. Now, the truth of the matter is, of course, really probably nobody thought anything of it. But we think people think something of it. That's why we own mirrors. If we weren't sensitive to this, we would just put on what we wanted and go. Here is an ad. This is not an information ad. This is, I'm not sure how old this is. But it's ancient. The photograph at the top is of a couple, a woman adoringly looking at him. And this guy over here, kind of pathetic, turned down again. We read your paragraph. The problem, by the way, is a thing called comadones. Does anybody know what comadones is? Anybody want to guess? Very good. Yeah, it's blackheads. What's more, you may not even be conscious of comadones. But your friends notice them. You may wonder why invitations become fewer, why friends, girls in particular seem to avoid you. You may never guess perhaps it's comadones. Pumpian massage cream helps you overcome comadones. Key words. You may wonder why invitations. See, you don't even know. This is how listerine was sold, to start with, by the way, too. That was an invented, the owners of listerine invented the condition. Here's a more contemporary ad, more recent, for an information product. Let me give you the secrets of fearless conversation. There's an endorsement here by O'Larry King, by the way. So, don't you hate it, the prospect of walking into a room that's packed with strangers, having to approach someone you've never met and strike up a conversation. It's natural to feel uncomfortable. You don't know. Are you making a positive first impression? Are you saying the right things? The answer is this course. It might interest you where this ad succeeded. Investors business daily. The Wall Street Journal. Forbes. Not trailer park monthly. Point being, personal anxiety and hypersensitivity to criticism. The desire not to be embarrassed. The desire not to be gossiped about. The desire not to feel foolish. The desire not to cling to the word is not exclusive to any demographic or catastrophe. The Forbes 400, the richest men and women in the world, get up every morning worrying about this stuff just like anybody else does. When they go into any environment that they are not comfortable with, what they want to know is how are you supposed to behave around here? probably which fork to use, but they go into all sorts of environments, they don't know the answer. One of the all-time classic ads in our industry is they all laughed when I sat down at the piano, but when I started to play. There's a version of this, I couldn't lay my hands on it, but there's a version of this. Capels also wrote it about, they all laughed when the waiter came over in the French restaurant, but then when I started to order in French. It's not exactly that, but it's pretty close. It's the same premise, being laughed at, not wanting to be laughed at. So the whole first part of this ad, this is all about how he's being goaded into playing and really they're goading him into play in order to make fun of him. The subhead says, then I started to play. Instantly a tense silence fell on the guests. The laughter died on their lips as if by magic. I played through the first few bars of Beethoven's Immortal Moonlight Sonata, yeah right, from the home study course. I heard gasps of amazement. My friends sat breathless, spellbound. The subhead here is a complete triumph. Now the truth of the matter is, who do you know who doesn't want to stick it up the butts of some friends of theirs over some skepticism those friends have about them? We all do. This is one of the most reliable emotions there is. This is a successful ad of mine. It will look familiar to some of you. The photograph is of a guy sitting in his underwear talking on a phone and it is the famous how you can make $4,000 a day sitting at your kitchen table in your underwear ad. I'll read you a piece of copy from it. I went from a young bright successful guy with a great job to an embarrassment to my family, a mooch to my friends. I sat in that basement after Peggy and the kids went to sleep on one hand feeling like finding a bridge and jumping on the other hand more determined than ever. This is what people want. They want to go from these negative emotional experiences to triumph. That's what they want. They want that much more than they want improvement for improvement's sake. They want it much more than they want opportunity for opportunity's sake. They want it much more than they want wellness for wellness's sake. They want to go from a collection of negative emotions and experiences to triumph. That's what we sell with our copy. Second key factor, emotional pain. Powerful sales copy inflicts emotional pain. We twist the knife. When we can figure out what a target market's emotional pain is, then we want to make it as painful for them as is humanly possible. A copy win is the person who read our copy and then could not sleep because of the angst we created. So you've all had an experience of intense emotional pain. You have failed miserably, for example, at something. You have embarrassed yourself somewhere. You have had a humiliating day or night and you come home and you can't get it out of your head. That's exactly where I want the reader. Then I of course want to solve it for him. So the methods of twisting the knife, here are the main ones. One is with an all too familiar scenario. Here's a favorite cartoon of mine. They're at the altar and she is saying that's what you're wearing. The headline is it starts. Isn't that an all too familiar scenario? I had a first wife who did that a lot. You're going out the door. Are you wearing that tie with that suit? No, I put it on for practice. No, I'm going to take it off in the car and go tireless. No, of course I'm, right before you went out the door. All too familiar scenario. Personal stories and confessions the reader sharply identifies with. This is copy for a client of mine who's a real estate coach who sells to other real estate agents. I'll show it to you and read it. It's in the middle of context of a lot of other copy. But the day that turned my life around, I looked at that trophy that said I was number one, but I felt like an absolute zero. I sat in a near dark room alone. Catherine upstairs asleep, a storm pushing rain against the window and occasionally illuminating the black sky with a momentary bolt of light. And I stared at that trophy and felt my hatred for it deepening, my shame in having it wrenching. I stared at that trophy and cried. I cursed and swore at that damn trophy. It lied. I wasn't number one. I was number zero because I still had to work at everything, as if starting out at zero the morning after being handed the trophy. And every morning thereafter I had to answer the same bell, do the same drudgery, face the same rejection. I still had to apologize for interrupting dinner for another quick call that took 20 minutes. I still had to apologize for another Saturday at work. I still had to worry about where and when the next commission check would materialize. I still started each month with certain bills, but uncertain income. I'd fought and clawed and worked day and night to get that trophy, but it changed nothing. You should applaud. No, I mean seriously, but that is a personal story that every successful real estate agent and pretty much any successful salesperson can identify with. They're getting accolades, recognition, award pins, trophies, praise, and yet inside nothing has changed. They're waking up in the morning facing the same, oh my god, where's the next deal coming from emotion. Now, it's embellished. Why? Because I want it embellished in his head. You could say it in two sentences, but if you're going to write good copy, you don't. You say it in two pages. You want to push him to despair. Third method, reminders of suppressed negative feelings about self. Here's copy from another client of mine. Actually, I'll give you two versions of this, reminders of suppressed negative feelings about self. I thought maybe they were right about me. Maybe I was in over my head. What the devil was I doing here sitting in the reception room of the president of a hundred million dollar company waiting to tell him what to do. What if he asked what university I graduated from? What if he called other executives in? I've never felt confident speaking to groups. Here's another version of the same copy. I thought maybe my father had been right all along. We are not the sort of people who ever rise to top incomes or live in the big homes in the best communities. What made me think I could make tens of thousands of dollars a month when my father barely made as much working all year? If it's in their heads, we wish to stimulate it for them. Triggering negative imagination of others' poor perceptions of them just showed you that in the previous ads. Third factor, also a dark one. Anger, rage and resentment. Getting people mad and then selling to them is a great approach to selling. Has anybody seen the Toronto Mayor guy who's smoking crack and drunk and he looks like Chris Farley on Saturday Night Live? There's a new video today. In addition to the crack smoking drunk video, this one he's like pretending to assault somebody and saying a bad word every three seconds and threatening the life of somebody and running around the room screaming manically. He is out of his mind with rage at whoever this person is and it is perhaps fueled by booze and crack as well. Here's one thing certain, simultaneous with that in those minutes. He is incapable of rational thought. He is incapable of anything, but his anger and rage. He is incapable of skepticism. He is incapable of analytical thought. He is incapable of critical thought. He is incapable of adding up value and comparing it to price. He is incapable of any of that. Mine can only hold one thing at a time by the way. So if we get them mad, they are incapable of analytical critical rational thought about the proposition that we put in front of them. They are only capable about being mad. So we have distracted them from all of the ways that they figure out how not to buy. So let me give you some examples. So this is boardroom copy. Boardroom is one of the largest, I showed you the book this morning. Boardroom is one of the largest info marketers there is. Newsletters and books. Bottom line health, bottom line personal, bottom line business. This is one of the great anger, rage headlines I've ever seen. It's boardrooms. The IRS has had you long enough. It would be great with a picture of a fist around somebody's testicles. That would be the perfect photo. That's probably a little much for boardroom. It wouldn't bother me to do it, but it's probably a little much for boardroom. The IRS has had you long enough. Siphoned out of our wallets is a piece of copy that appears. I didn't give you the whole page, but and for what? So wasteful bureaucrats can throw our money down a rat hole. So money-grubbing politicians can use it to pay political favors. So IRS agents can hound you and harass you and treat you as guilty until proven innocent. I say enough. Yeah. And so this is by the way what it looks like. It's got a little picture of Marty Edelston who just passed away, just left us standing there with arms folded and the IRS has had you long enough. The solution is a book of course with 5,022 ways that you can reduce your taxes and deprive the IRS of money. But if I get you mad enough at the IRS, you do not do a lot of critical thinking about how many of those 5,022 ways apply to you, how many are legal, what your CPA might think about them. So you don't think about any of that. You're just mad and you act on being mad. How can you now act on being made mad? Well, you can't go grab your baseball bat out of your closet and go beat up an IRS agent. All right? How can you, well, no, it's not a bad idea, but how can you, so the way you can act on the rage it has been created is by the book. And you immediately feel better when you buy the book because you did something about being had long enough. Understand that in our world purchase counts as accomplishment. And it is in our best interest to let that confusion occur. So the person who gets whipped up by this copy and is mad as hell at the IRS and the money grubbing politicians and acts on his rage by ordering the book immediately feels better. Not as good as baseball bat, but he feels better because he has done something finally about this deplorable situation. Has he really done anything? No. Has he changed anything? No. Has he accomplished anything? No. Is he likely to? No. 90% of the books will never have the straight crap taken off of them. But at that moment in time, purchase was the same as accomplishment. It is in our best interest to let that be. Fourth factor, the one we'll spend the most time on. It is not all that dark. It is just fundamentally weird. And it is fascinating that we all operate this way. And everyone here will deny you do. But we all operate this way. So the fourth factor that you can use to drive a lot of business in our world is mysticism. And all of its various forms. So overwhelming majorities of our population believe in secrets. They believe in secret societies. They believe in conspiracies. They believe that there is some secret being withheld from them that if they just had that, so fat people believe, no disrespect, any fat folk in the room, if it makes you feel any better, I once was one of you. Fat people believe in all sorts of secrets and secret conspiracies. They believe that there are ingredients put into the food deliberately stuck in there to make people fat. They believe that there are diet secrets, skinny people know, that are withheld from them, and not told to them. They believe all sorts of these things. Here is why they must. You can't help yourself if you're fat. From believing there must be a conspiracy, a secret. If you are poor, poor people believe that there are secrets deliberately withheld from them by rich people, that there is an entire conspiracy societally and politically structured to keep them poor. They must believe that. What is the alternative belief? I'm a loser. I'm a big fat person with zero discipline, no sense, who cannot manage myself at all. I'm a broke person because I am willfully stupid, lazy, useless, pitiful, and pathetic. This is an unappealing proposition. A much more appealing proposition is that there's a conspiracy to keep me fat and poor. Mysticism exists because it's a viable and preferable alternative to reality for most people in many categories. Just so you have a handle, I have some facts for you. Aliens from outer space. There are currently over 500 different organizations that people who believe that there are aliens from outer space among us that they belong to. In aggregate, they have 16 million members. There is, for example, the ancient astronaut society, which of course believes in the existence of ancient astronauts. They each pay $139 a year in dues to belong to the ancient astronaut society. The percentage of people in general, according to Rasmussen surveys, who believe in aliens among us. If you do a lot of coaching, it's easy to believe, by the way, in aliens among us. It explains a great deal. 32% believe in aliens among us. Percentage of people who believe in government cover-ups of aliens among us is 47%. Curiously, that's higher than the percentage of the people. I'm not quite sure of that math. Political conspiracies. JFK assassination, fake moon landing, 9-11 truthers, Obama Berthers, Bilderbergs, one world order, secret U.S. internment camps, death panels. Every single one of these things, it's more than 30% of the population believes them. I don't know if it's the same 30%. I don't know that. Actually, there are entire industries around this. If you don't know this, there are speaking circuits. There are magazines. There are newsletters. There are products. There are DVDs. There are CDs. There's an entire industry just around political conspiracies. We have mystical powers. December 30th, year 2000. Anybody here from New Orleans? No? December 30th, year 2000, the New Orleans Saints, the NFL team in New Orleans, for those of you who don't follow football, paid a voodoo priestess to perform a pre-game ritual to free the team from curses. New Orleans then won its first playoff game in its team history, defeating the Rams. Somebody please text the Cleveland Browns and the Jacksonville Jaguars. The percentage of adults who believe in ghosts, 34%. American Society of Psychical Research, which is research into the paranormal, like Ghostbusters. It has 9,000 volumes of research and has received over $50 million in grants from benefactors. Mystical powers. This is a sales letter, not from a real weird source. How many of you in here know what Nightingale Conan is? Raise your hands. How many people bought something from Nightingale Conan? This is a Nightingale Conan sales letter and a successful one. Journey into the fourth dimension. Scientists are baffled. How is it that some people are able to gain access to information completely outside their physical experience? In this report, you will discover exactly how the CIA, the Stanford Research Institute, and others have done it. Plus, you'll learn how you can do it for yourself. Subhead. Everyone has psychic ability. Better subhead. Remote viewing. Remote viewing. Proof of non-local universal consciousness. This is a 16-page sales letter for an audio cassette program that teaches you how to separate your mind from your body and get psychic insight that you can use to be more successful in life and in business. This is a Nightingale Conan sales letter. This is not some bizarre entity, some person in a cave somewhere. So, this is going to the Nightingale Conan list with a lot of people on that list like you. None of whom would ever admit to buying it. But a whole lot of people own it. Mystical powers. How many of you follow Agora? Good. One of the largest info marketers, mostly in financial information, almost all of which is based around some sort of secret formula. There's a, how many of you know what the tout industry is? Not many. They lie, Bill. They lie. But the tout industry does roughly a billion dollars a year in business. Almost 50% of it is right now during the football season because more people bet on that than of any other sports. But it's a year-round industry. It is, so how many know who Jim Kramer is on CNBC who picks stocks? Okay. So, think of somebody just as disreputable and foolish as is Jim Kramer, except instead of telling you what stock to buy, they tell you which team to bet on in the Monday night game. And people pay them money for this inside information and expert prediction about which team to bet on. And people pay this industry a billion dollars a year to tell them whether the Rams will cover the spread on Thursday night. It is utter crap from beginning to end. In fact, most of them, dirty little secret, because they sell their customers yearly plans, $3,000 plan, $5,000 plan, $10,000 plan. They start the season by dragging you to a free game selection and they get 5,000 people to call in and they give 2,500 on one side of the game, 2,500 on the other side of the game. On Monday, they got 2,500 winners. They split them again on Monday night, give 1,250 one side of the game, 1,250 the other side of the game. On Tuesday morning, they have 1,250 people who just went two for two. That's the business. Kramer's business isn't much better. There's a guy called the Wall Street Psychic who from 2005 to 2008 was paid over $6 million for consulting with executives of leading banks and financial firms, making psychic predictions about what would happen in the economy. Mystical powers. Tony's Fire Walk. How many of you have done the Fire Walk? Good for you. How many of you know it's a carnival trick from the 1800s? Okay, good. I'm glad it worked. Well, of course it works. It's a carnival trick. It works. Otherwise, it couldn't be a carnival trick. What do you mean it works? Of course it works, but it's a trick. It is no mystical power, not by the person putting you through it and not by you. Hate to disillusion you, but you have no mystical powers evidenced by the fact that you can walk across the hot coals without burning your feet. It has nothing to do with you or your feet. It has an enormous amount to do with the trick itself. The Wayne Dyer School of Magical Thinking. I'm in a meeting with Wayne, with some infomercial executives, as he is explaining to them how he is able to be in three places at the same time. One executive after the other went to the restroom, none of them ever came back. I said it might have been better to keep that little tidbit to yourself. I have a line on here. I shouldn't do it, but I put it down. I thought so. The line is Sacred Cows, Magic Underwear, and 77 Virgins. So everybody's religion flirts with mystical powers, suppressed cures. There is a whole industry built around the idea that there are cures exist for virtually every disease you wish to name, particularly cancer and AIDS, that are being suppressed by a conspiracy of, you name it, the FDA and Big Pharma. There are DVDs selling all about the engines that run on water, the engines that run on catch it, the engines that run on everything but gasoline, that GM has bought up in order to lock them away in a cabinet and not let anybody, self-generating electricity, a machine that makes its own electricity. This scam has been run over 500 recorded times, where investors have been brought into rooms and shown a machine that generates its own electricity. The first recorded one was in the 1800s. The most recent one was only three years ago to the tune of 20-some-odd million dollars. Fountain of Youth. Syrogenics, kyrrogenics, however you say it, the deal where they freeze you, if you haven't heard this pitch, by the way, and I realize now I run the risk of insulting somebody. You don't also believe in being frozen, do you? Two, okay, of course. Gotta be in the front row. Gotta be in the front row. Well, you'll love this. Let me deviate for a second. This is, wait till you hear this pitch. Wait till you hear this pitch. So I'll preface it by, I love the network marketing industry dearly, however. So up until this, the pitch at which I could not keep a straight face. I was in the back of the room the first time I heard it, and because I liked the people doing it, I ran from the room before I burst out laughing and ruined their whole meeting because they were in a bee pollen business. And the problem with the bee pollen business, if you don't know, is that it comes by taking the stuff off the feet of the bees and the bees die when you take the stuff. And nobody really likes a specter of dying bees. So they have their scientist on stage showing a presentation about how to make their bee pollen. They have a patented process, and they have this machine that the bees go through, and it only takes the pollen from the left feet of the bees. Thereby, no bees die in the making of this product. Now, the guy who can deliver this with a straight face, I mean, this is talent. But I now found something that trumps it. So this may look like ordinary bottled water to you, but oh no, it isn't. This is H2M water, and there's five different kinds. This is prosperity water. This is perfect health water. The slogan is, think it while you drink it. Way do you hear the pitch? This is vibration hydration. While prosperity water is being bottled, everybody who is bottling it is chanting a prosperity chant. Hence, water. Infusing the water with prosperity energy so that when you drink it, you automatically, with no effort, no work, no change in behavior, become prosperous. If you don't need to be prosperous, perfect health water. No exercise, no diet, no nothing. They all chanted while the perfect health chant, while they bottled the water, and now all you got to do is drink the water. It's only 10 bucks a bottle. Miracle cures. Sharks don't eat cancer. How many of you remember that, baby? Sharks don't get cancer. Therefore, if you eat shark cartilage, you won't get cancer. On your own time, dissect the complete illogic of that statement, which for many years sold tens of millions of dollars of shark cartilage. My dog does not shed. So if I ate my dog, I would still have a full head of hair, and no hair would have fallen out. Same logic. Exact same logic. Miracle cures. Dr. Brinkley, who Chip and I wrote about in making them believe the book and making them believe home study course, was the richest doctor of his time. The AMA was invented to put him out of business if you don't know the story. He had people coming on trains from all across the country to his clinic, spending at the time, roughly today's equivalent would be $75,000 for an operation, to have goat testicles grafted to their testicles to cure their erectile dysfunction. Graffited to, understand, we take the goat testicle, this is by the way very bad news for goats. Almost every proposition is good news for somebody, bad news for somebody. This is like stapling it to the outside of your testicle. This is ladies, this is the rough equivalent of a topical that makes Veracose veins go away. No such thing. It's internal problem, not an external problem. However, this preposterous cure, the first known recorded cure being sold for erectile dysfunction, if you go read the Brinkley story, if you want the whole story there's a book not ours called charlatan which is a great read and that our book is all about the marketing of Brinkley and he was way ahead of his time in many respects. They came from all across the country. Guess what 20% of his clients were? Good for you. Doctors. Doctors coming to have goat testicles grafted to their testicles to cure their erectile dysfunction. Miracle cures. Miscellaneous. The Flat Earth Society, which was founded in 1800, has of 1992 has 28,000 dues paying members. The amount bid on eBay for a grilled cheese sandwich bearing the image of the Virgin Mary was 28,000 dollars. 2000 people per hour came in June of 1997 to see the image of the Virgin Mary in a puddle at the subway station in Mexico City. Financial. I remind you of Bernie Madoff. A lot of people think that's new. If you want some fun sometime, Google home steak oil swindle and get the book which has the list of everybody who invested in home steak oil, which was another Ponzi scheme, much like Madoff, the list is rich with people who should know better, including at the time the Secretary of the U.S. Treasury, who invested in home steak oil, which was every bit as unexplainable as Enron. The Enron story was actually taken from the home steak oil story. They just substituted electricity for oil. In order to sell home steak oil, they went out in the middle of the desert outside of Palm Springs and they put purple pipes, white pipes, green pipes, turquoise pipes, plastic pipes out of the desert all crisscrossing each other and they flew people over them in helicopters and said, see? And everybody signed checks. In 1999, a TV commercial was produced for Whiskas Cat Food with embedded subliminal messages for the cat. A CEO approved this. Percentage of the U.S. population that believes Elvis faked his death, 47%. I have a thing, you can't laugh at that. If you're fire walking and getting your head froze so that after you die you can come back when your body's diseases are cured, you cannot laugh at the 47% of the people who believe Elvis faked his death. You are forbidden. Okay, of course. Okay, there we go. Well, so you'll like my theory about it then. My theory is after his fake death, he spent his life working as an Elvis impersonator at Holiday Dance. It's called Hiding in Plain Sight. Well, we could go on. People believe this. It's one of the 100 most powerful headlines in advertising. It never wears out because people believe in this stuff. So I'll show you some copy. It happens to be mine. This is current. The IRS hopes you never read this. This is an important commonality. It's been in every example I've shown you. There is somebody hoping and praying that you never hear about X. That's an important copy piece. Here it is again just said differently. Legal loopholes your accountant never tells you about relying on your tax preparer to save you money. I never thought this would work but it did. I tried it into my surprise. I got 34 silver coins from a single bank. Needless to say I'm going back for more. Thanks for the great idea. The most amazing means of making spare time, moonlighting or weekend income ever invented that's hidden in plain sight on every street in America, which is why we call it hidden in plain sight money. This is kept behind a curtain where insiders easily pocket finders fees. Get this mental picture. Step into a continuous flow of hidden in plain sight money and receive large paychecks sent by Federal Express right to your door. Well, who wouldn't want that? The mental picture of the FedEx guy bringing you money suspends all rational thought. Who wouldn't want that? This is mailed to investor lists, people that have accounts in which they buy and sell and trade stocks. Ignoring my last two alerts cost you quick profits of 589% and 610%. A secret plan for veterans to get $1,949 per month tax free. It's criminal that veterans aren't better informed of these benefits that they deserve. Warning while your life insurance may be a sitting duck for taxes and how to fix it. Warning the hidden mistake with revocable trust that traps your money. Insurance salesmen sell insurance. Lawyers sell trust. Beware. The truth about gold, you see the TV ads, hear the radio ads, what you must know before you buy gold or if you already own it, that they won't tell you. It never wears out. The secrets that they don't want you to know. Factor five, revenge. An enormously popular concept. It's in the all laughed ad I showed you. Until I started to play, then they all shut up, their jaws dropped, they were amazed. That's revenge. Who doesn't want revenge against someone? A former girlfriend remembers Bill Gates as being boring and having bad breath. He remembers her as not having $100 million. Who doesn't want revenge? By somebody, your buyer feels underestimated. Thought of as such things are not for you. Disrespected, doubted, skeptical about. Looked down upon, put down, treated as a second class citizen and excluded. By somebody, everybody feels this. Trump's father's advice was stick to small properties in the boroughs. The big city is too much for you. Have you watched Leno lately? He is not going happily. He feels very disrespected by NBC. Somebody could sell him on doing something not smart, like a show on Fox when he leaves. These feelings are held by everybody. They feel it about someone's response to them and they wish to prove them wrong. Story. So revenge stories work very well in copy. The most raucous standing ovation I have ever seen and I have seen many was the delivery of a beautifully scripted story which I will not do verbatim for you. But in my youth, I was at a large Amway rally. For those of you that don't know, the Amway system for many years included a monthly area rally. And typically a superstar of the business was the featured speaker. This guy's name was Charlie Marsh. And he was a kind of ugly, crew cut, toady looking guy, a ex cop. He had a good looking wife. But Charlie didn't look like much. And Charlie told the story. Go all the way back also to the point of this cartoon. So Charlie told the story of his first, so how many of you have been in an MLM? Raise your hands. How many of you have been in an MLM that uses living room opportunity meetings? So you would all identify with this story. So Charlie told the story of getting in the business and of course having his wife very skeptical and his mother-in-law very skeptical. And Charlie had a history sort of like Jackie Gleason on the honeymooners and Al Bundy of trying one scheme after another to make money and failing miserably. And so here we go again. And so Charlie invites, as everyone was taught to do, to his first living room meeting, all his friends, his cop buddies and his neighbors and relatives. And at seven o'clock his living room is empty and his nose is pressed to the window waiting for someone to come and they do not come. And then next week he called and invited everybody again to another meeting and they did not come. Now he tells this story much more poignantly than I am telling. It leads up to hitting Crown Directorstributor, which at the time was the highest level in Amoy, and taking his wife on a 21-day world cruise. And inviting everybody down to the docks, to the Bon Voyage Party, all the friends, the relatives, the neighbors, his cop buddies, everybody to a Bon Voyage Party on the docks, to which everybody came because there was free food, free booze, balloons, etc. However, they were already on the boat so that they could stay in by the railing of the boat as all their friends gathered on the docks and give them the finger. I have never seen an audience, now it's perfect message to market match, I have never in all my years seen an audience erupt and stay erupted for as long a period of time as that story because every one of them in the room shared the first part of that experience. And it hurts. It is emotional pain, not just a business occurrence. And Charlie was revenge. And everybody went home that night thinking about what their version of the Bon Voyage Party would be. Larry King tells the story, which again I will condense about when he was on radio in Miami, which was early in his career, getting a call from an Italian accent bent-nosed guy explaining to him that they were having a benefit for the local cardinal and they wanted Larry to MC and Larry agreed. And when he got there, the bent-nosed guys took him backstage and explained to Larry there's only a few rules, no jokes about the mafia because there's no such thing as a mafia. Don't ask anybody to turn the house lights up because there's competitors in the room and there's some folks from the government in the back and everybody kind of knows everybody's here but they don't like to see everybody. So just don't ask everybody to turn up the lights and please finish on time. So Larry does this thing and all he wants to do is get out of there. And they are in the back waiting for him and everybody's very pleased, did a very nice job, we're very grateful and now we owe you a favor. Larry just wants to get out of there. And they said no you don't understand, we owe you a favor and we don't like owing people favors. So what would you like? Larry said, he says the next question you'll probably never be asking in your life. He said, but the next question out of the guy's mouth to me is, well you got anybody you want whacked? Larry says, I don't care who you are, at that moment in your mind you start a list. All right. Now, the same thing with Charlie's Bon Voyage story. What occupied their minds for the rest of the evening was their version of the Bon Voyage party. And even people not yet in the business have an experience like that where they have been dissed by their family and their friends and their co-workers and boy they would like vindication. That story now presses everything else out of their little pointy heads. So there's no sales resistance to what is being sold. It's a very important principle. We'll talk on the last day about the genealogy precursor to Jake, Charles Atlas. And of course Charles Atlas has made his fortune. The people around him made their fortune. One of the most famous of all vindication stories about the bully who kicks sand in the face. A version of it from an old friend of mine and I want to read pieces of it to you because it is so good. This is a guy by name Ross Jeffries. How many of you know Ross? Not many. So Ross is a guy no woman in the room would like which is part of his shtick. He's not quite as bad as he and I am about to make him sound. But in his day Ross was on Phil Donahue. He was on all the daytime talk shows because Ross was one of the early people in the genre in our industry of teaching men how to pick up and have sex fast with women. Not the build a relationship category here. This is not christianmingle.com. This is amazing seduction secrets of a skinny ugly six foot geek from Culver City, California that could get you all the girls you want no matter what your looks or age. The ad looks like this. Ran in all sorts of magazines. The word secret appears in it six times. I'm going to just read pieces. But in the right place there's a whole lot of yeses to that question. In private, no man in this room sitting anywhere near an accompanying woman would respond. You've all been looking down looking up but a lot of yeses in privacy. Next question, would you like to be able to get a woman into bed fast and easy even if she already has a serious boyfriend? Next question, oh it gets worse. How about getting that girl who's been your longtime friend into bed within two hours just by using a simple easy technique? I'm the skinny ugly geek in the headline and you may have seen me on nationwide TV shows like Donahue and Geraldo teaching thousands of men how to be more successful with women. On these shows I talked about my original classic book how to get the women you desire into bed but now I've come up with something new and better guaranteed to get any guy scoring. This entire pitch is about a magic mental technique that is irresistible that you can learn that now it doesn't matter. You can be a troll and you probably can't see very well. You can be a troll and you get that. By the way interesting ad technique the caption drives right to a recorded message. The caption under her is what would it be worth to you to be able to have as many beautiful women as you wanted whenever you wanted them. Call 310-358-78 for a free recorded message to learn how. I'll just read a couple subheads for you. Here are just a few of the secrets you'll learn. How to have her begging for it despite a boyfriend. We call this one the boyfriend destroyer. How to turn 20 minutes over coffee into hours of heated passion. You'll never have to go out on a date again. I can't do the other two. It's just I just I just can't do it. Now to a great degree this is the B-Poll. I mean you know if you were going to deliver this to the stage being able to do it with a straight face is a remarkable talent. You would think of course that this would have to be in some horrible publications below Hustler read by trailer park Cretans but again you would be wrong. Amongst the publications it works in would be investors business daily. Factor number six fear slash insecurity. There are many fears and everybody has at least one. The most common are fear of the future fear of change fear of loss fear of harm fear of a powerful enemy fear of regret fear of ill health and infirmity and then every specific target audience has its own specific fears. Hospital executives right now for example are scared to death of the uncertain and unknown ramifications of Obamacare. That's a specific fear but these general ones apply at different times to just about all of us. So let's do a demonstration. We're going to vote on headlines and you will tell me which of these you think I'm going to show you seven are more powerful. They are headlines that could be used to sell some sort of information product about relationships to women. So the audience is women. Here are the headlines. I'll let you look for a second and then we'll vote because you learn more by looking around at everybody else and you learn me. Okay so headline number one is improve your home life. Let's see the votes. Hardly any improve your marriage. Improve your love life. Got some hands there. Improve your sex life. Got more hands there. Tell us something about the audience. How to keep your husband interested in you even after years of marriage. More hands. Love secrets. How to keep your husband interested in you even after years of marriage. A lot more hands. So that's the one you all like. That's it. Okay and the last one secrets husbands wish their wives knew how to keep your husband interested in you even after years of marriage. Still number six is the winner here by the way. Now now are any of those has powerful as tell tale signs how to decode the secret clues that your husband is losing interest in you. Tell tale signs how to decode the secret clues that your husband is becoming interested in another woman. Tell tale signs how to decode the secret clues that your husband is thinking about asking for a divorce. If you had to bet a hundred thousand dollars on an ad campaign and you could choose any of the headlines you just saw from the former page or from this page and you could only bet on one which page would you pick a headline from. This page you better believe it and if any of you think otherwise you are sharing the crack pipe with the mayor from Toronto. If you would like to know these are real headlines from real split tests and the winner is divorce. Why because it encompasses the most fears. Go back to our fear list. Fear of the future. Fear of change. Fear of loss. Fear of harm. Fear of a powerful enemy. Why is he getting divorced. Another woman. Fear of regret. Fear of ill health and infirmity. We got almost all of them except maybe the last right. So the winner is the divorce headline. None of the ones on the first page come even close to the ones on the second page. Positive motivation. Negative motivation. Which has more power. Negative motivation. A brief diversion. Many of you won't like that. I don't actually like it either. I would rather just use positive motivation. The time to use positive motivation is after you have a customer and you are now helping them. That's where it belongs. After the acquisition. After the sale. Not before the acquisition and not before the sale. All it does there is get in your way. So many of you won't like it. If it is any consolation to you whatsoever I don't like it either. I'm really a warm fuzzy happy cheery optimistic guy. I am. I'm a little furball. But I also like to sell stuff. The second little diversion is some of you will choose not to believe it about your customers. Your target market. You will say well maybe but my people are not like this. My people are smarter. My people are better educated. My people are more sophisticated. I've tried to drive that home to you by mentioning how many of these ads work in investors business daily. Go get the ad rate card if you'd like to see the demographics. They're very high. College educated advanced degrees. High incomes etc. But you will still say not my people. My people are different. And you will be wrong. And why do you believe that if in fact that thought popped into your mind. Now because it's your ego talking. It's flattering to have customers who are smarter more sophisticated and don't behave like this. You want to believe that about your customers. You certainly want to believe it about yourself. However we all operate this way. Situationally at different times we all operate this way. Everybody believes themselves and their customers are different and better. Too good for this. You would be wrong. Anxiety and security. So this is a this is the top half of a postcard very successful for an ordinary financial advisor. Say oh oh. Okay. If only you could feel this secure and worry free as the cat. Now this works for an ordinary financial advisor. So this postcard is mailing to a demographic list in a target market area of people that might only have two hundred thousand dollars. The ad I'm about to show you is running in Forbes. And is it requires response only from someone who has sold or is in the process of selling their company for at least ten million dollars. Would you agree that that person this person who is has sold or is about to sell their company for ten million dollars or more is probably smarter more educated more sophisticated saying or more rational than the ordinary rank and file perhaps blue collar person who might respond to this. No. She said yes. No. No. They're not. They appear to be. They got a better suit on. They live in a better house. The only difference between people who have a lot of money and no money is the people that have a lot of money have a lot of money. That's it. That's the only difference. Years ago Wesley Snipes you guys only know who Wesley Snipes is. So Wesley Snipes is being interviewed on a today show. This is before he was a tax cheat and a felon and all that stuff. So he's being interviewed on a today show. And I don't remember now who the host was. They're all a blur. You know. But the host asked him this question. Everybody's the same. I don't care if they read Forbes or they read trailer park monthly. I got news for you. They both checked their astrology forecast. I did an infomercial with Nancy Reagan's astrologer. What a whack job. No offense intended. I'm sure. Hey Dave are you in a room anywhere. This is the one to do a psychic reading for right here. You can't mess this up. So most people though would nod like you did. Oh of course the person who reads this must be smarter. More sophisticated. More rational. More everything than the person with just 200,000 dollars living in a little retirement community in Arizona. Everybody would agree and everybody would be wrong. Here's the ad. When a man sells his company conflicting advice flies vulture circle unseen peril lurks. Who can really be trusted to tell him the truth. The guy who wrote the book. If you intend to sell or selling or have recently sold a company worth 10 million to 40 million dollars or more I have vital experience based information that even your most trusted advisors may not tell you. And may not even know. See if I can find another one. It's all in here by the way. You are obviously an accomplished successful person or you would not have a valuable entity to sell but you are not experienced in this and the intermediaries and advisors directly involved in this transaction have agendas of their own. You will have or now have a large sum of money in a low yield environment. You may find yourself feeling lost and overly eager to get back into an active decision making role. You will confront conflicting advice issues with people in your life and numerous opportunities. If not careful you will put money that can no longer be replaced into situations with unacceptable perhaps hidden and secret risks. I've seen very successful and smart people make critical mistakes during and after the sale of a business and I can present those hazards for your consideration and avoidance. There are a surprising number of pitfalls including some concealed from view during the process of selling a company. I've only read you pieces. There is only nuance language differences between this and all the other fear stuff I have shown you. Forbes Reader who has sold a company for 10 to 40 million dollars. Seventh factor greed and sloth it never goes out of style. The desire for something for nothing particularly money is universal. The 1960s the big hit in our industry. Self published book full page newspaper ads it's one of the great classic ads was Joe Carbo's The Lazy Man's Way to Riches. Full page ads ran on the back pages of major metropolitan newspapers and business sections of newspapers all across the country. Not the newspaper in Toothlessville, Alabama, the New York Times, the LA Times, the Washington Post, the Chicago Tribune for the lazy man's way to riches. Anyone with enough brains to actually read a copy rich full page newspaper ad if they are engaged in rational thought knows the promise can't be true. Almost everybody knows at least one rich person and they read about rich people and you can't find a lazy rich person. It's impossible. Even the third generation ones who inherit it aren't lazy. They're at every party nightclub boat show blowing money like you can't imagine. You can't do that sitting on a couch eating Cheetos. You cannot burn through a billion dollar fortune in a single generation being lazy about it. You got to work at blowing that money. You will never meet a lazy rich person. In fact, most rich people work harder and work longer hours than most poor people. Furthermore, they turn everything they do into unlazy activity. Warren Buffett decides to play bridge. He treks and finds the number one bridge player in the country and pays her a ton of money to teach him how to play bridge so he can win. That's how rich people have hobbies. Two reasons I don't play golf. One is the story Dave alluded to which I've been not last night with one of the groups which I'm not going to tell you. I don't think. But the other reason is because if I did, if I did, it would be barely a matter of months before I would have three coaches, be scheming to get on the senior tour, own a country club and be immersed in golf as work. I could not help myself. That's what we are. So anyone who can read a full page newspaper ad, engaged in rational thought, knows this is not true. However, everybody wants it. Everybody would like to know about it if it did exist. And if we can get somebody to suspend rational thought long enough, they will buy it to find out about it. You may recall very successful management book, 1980s, the one minute manager. No one published the one minute lover. But the one minute manager was a big hit. How many of you have ever managed anybody? Child, employee, anybody? How many of you if engaged in rational thought think that you can do it in one minute a day? Lots of luck. But we'd like to and if. My friend Tim Ferriss, Google ad words test driven title. The book is a lot better than the title. The four hour work week. We've now come from 1960 to now. Nobody's gotten any smarter. Four hour work week. Really? Really? Homeless people spend more than four hours at the street corner with their sign. No one. No one has a four hour work week. While driving the book out of the best seller list, by the way, my friend Tim was flying across the country to go do interviews on the Today Show, doing radio interviews from dawn until midnight, seven days a week, hustle, hustle, hustle, hustle, hustle, hustle, hustle. Seemingly you could drive the four hour work week book to the best seller list with four hour work weeks. No, you can't. But we would like this to be true. And if we could get somebody to suspend rational thought long enough, they will buy it to find out whether or not it can possibly be true. One of the all time hits headlines is lose weight while you sleep, make money while you sleep. You could do it either way. It's been used in both ways. It's not new. These headlines have a 50 year history of being trotted out and used again and again and again. Lose weight while you sleep. You got to have restless leg syndrome beyond anyone's imagination to burn off a few calories while you sleep. I sleep fast, but I don't lose it anyway. And anyone with a brain knows how many have seen the current ad campaign for the stuff you sprinkle on your food so none of the calories and the fat grams stick to you when they go through your system. How many of you got the stuff? Come on, be proud. 50 million people have bought it. Odds are there's one of you in the room. Here's how un-knew that is. 1963, full page ad, National Enquirer. Had a banana split this big and a gallon of bikini with a 21 inch waist next to it shrunk down so the banana split was bigger than she was. Sprinkling powder on the banana split because if you put this powder on it you could eat as many of them as you want and not gain any weight or inches. 1963, this is 2013. This never goes out of style. We all know better. Doesn't matter. We all hope it is possible. Greed and sloth. So here's some copy. This is all copy I've written at various times for different promotions. It does the work for you. It does the heavy lifting for you. For you are two really good words. It implies you do nothing and you get a result. Easier than any way of making money you've ever seen. Make $1,000 a day in less time than it takes to shower and dress in the morning. Guess what that does? That's the trigger mental picture again. That headline makes somebody think about taking a shower and getting dressed and thinking about how many minutes they take to do that and how cool it would be to make $1,000 in that amount of time and while they're thinking that they can't think anything else. If you can click a mouse, microwave money as fast as microwaving a cup of coffee. Same thing. Guy pictures himself in front of the microwave waiting for the coffee. Which by the way we all do that. It takes what? A minute and we are impatiently standing there waiting for the coffee. A minute. You think by now we can get a man to the moon. You think we could reduce this a little bit. Mail just two type pages to any 25 of your customers every day and watch a tidal wave of extra sales flow in. The magic in that is pretty much everybody thinks they can handle getting two type pages into an envelope. Push button profits. This is a letter to get joint ventures. We first year's did in 2000. Looks like this. Still use it today so it's 13 years old. Clients using it today. Works as an email too by the way. Regarding free money. Dear Hoosies, I'm interested in setting up a joint venture with you offering to your customers. It will be very simple way for you to add substantial profits to your bottom line without lifting a finger or investing a dime. I will do all the work, pay all the costs, and make sure the project is kept on track. All you have to do is give me the okay to move forward and deposit the $1000 checks I will send you. This will get people on the phone. If it is FedEx with a $100 bill attached to it, which is how we use it, my client uses it, it really gets people on the phone. And yet if they stopped and thought about it and they tapped their own business experience, because this is going to successful business people in a particular industry, peers, they know there's no such thing as free money. However, who doesn't want free money? The combination of this promise, $100 bill attached to it, they can't think of anything else. Rational thought is gone. Critical thought is gone. Hand is going to phone. Mind going nowhere. Here is a variation on greed and sloth. This will make you a lot of money, this one. So be sure you leave it this one. This is called the how to get everything you want without doing anything you don't like or don't want to do. That's a template for copy. All you have to do is turn the generics into specifics that match your market. How to get the exact body you want without doing any exercise without, et cetera. I call it the pizza and ice cream diet. It's all the weight you want on pizza and ice cream. The key word here is the word without. For you, two key words, without key word. Here it is in actual use. This is a current piece for a client of mine. By the way, it's aimed at dentists. How many dentists do we have in the room? Great. How many years you've got to go to college to be a dentist? Eight years to go to college to be a dentist. So get this, he's got eight years of college. Here's what I'm putting in front of him. What if you really could double production with only five elegantly simple but profoundly powerful changes, put in place in just days without spending one red cent more on external advertising or marketing, without leasing the next new pricey techno gadget, without adding to staff or piling new marketing work on to them and facing mutiny, and without you having to become some sort of green-eyed monster, super salesman and mastercloser of giant cases. That is how to get everything you want without doing anything you don't like or don't like to do. That's what it is. He has eight years of college. It will fill a seminar room. I'll give you the longer list. You'll even like the longer list better. Watch this. So look how long the list is. I'll read it. At least double your present production. See, significant production increases the first two months without doubling or even sharply increasing your workload. In fact, you'll probably wind up working less without doubling your overhead. In fact, you will raise profitability of your practice. It's common for net to go down as gross goes up. We will not put you on that insane trajectory without doubling your advertising or marketing budget or activity without sacrificing your professionalism, your ethics or your reputation, without you becoming a super salesman or mastercloser, without asking your hygienists to think and act like a car salesman, without pressuring staff to think and act in ways they find insulting or unpleasant or overly resistant or want to sabotage, without any new pricey technology, without piling marketing work on your staff. You will finally create income certainty, predictable study, new patient flow you can depend on by making only five, five is good because five fingers. Only five, elegantly, what's better than six, say six is a, five is a much better number, by making only five elegantly simple changes, all of which can be fully and completely understood in two days. You will sustain positive staff pride and morale and real teamwork and strengthen the entire practice. That's a good list, isn't it? Isn't that a good list? You bet it's a good list. So, these seven factors thrive our industry. For them to be fair, what you are selling with them must be able to actually deliver on the promise and must be beneficial to the people to whom you are selling it. You decide for yourself whether you are playing fair or not or whether you wish to play fair. But these are the seven factors that drive our industry. These seven factors embedded in your sales copy, in your sales scripts, in your webinar script, in your personal presentation. So, I have a homework assignment for you. There will be no test. You have, you should have had on your seat, amongst other things, a thing that looks like this and you should have another one I don't have that's white. Hey, some of them got them, they're waving them. They were on everybody's seat when they came in. If you didn't get it, see that one, thank you very much. May I have that for just a minute so people know what they're supposed to be looking for? Thank you. You should have one that looks like this. Well, then go see customer service when we break. Somebody took it from you because it was on every seat before the room opened. Thank you. It's in the envelope. Is there an envelope on your seat? Okay. She says yes there is. So, she will come over there and make it materialized as soon as she levitates herself across the coals. Anyway, you are looking at these from somebody. Mug the person next to you, go back to customer service if they're not on your chair. Okay. The assignment, besides, perhaps you might want to register for one or the other of them, they are my trainings coming up in June. The one with the yellow is directly relevant to this topic because it is all about the craftsmanship of one too many selling presentations by any means. However, whether you wish to register or not, a good homework exercise now is to read these and see how many of the seven factors you can find and the specific copy tactics I gave you that you could find used throughout these pieces. You will find, ah, a person passing out more envelopes. You may stay. You don't need to levitate. They have solved the problem. It's witchcraft. So, a good exercise is to see how good a job I did with what I have now shown you. That will be a very good exercise. Tomorrow morning, we resume and I will show you some very specific copy techniques that build on these seven factors and utilize these seven factors. I will not go back and point out the seven factors. Again, we will not redo the evening. We will do something new. And I will show you specific copy techniques and a lot of samples of other materials that build on these seven factors. And I believe we begin properly at eight o'clock tomorrow morning. Please be here on time and we are done for the evening. Thank you.
